title: Tanacetum argyranthemoides
intro: Tanacetum argyranthemoides, also known as Pyrethrum or Dalmatian Chrysanthemum, is a perennial plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Tanacetum argyranthemoides include pyrethrin compounds, which are responsible for its insecticidal and antiseptic properties, as well as sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids. The plant is commonly prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used in traditional medicine to treat a range of conditions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
taxonomy: Tanacetum argyranthemoides is classified in the family Asteraceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family.
